stories.title: Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges
stories.description: The president has had mixed messages about how and when the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran will end.

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants, bridges. And, Artemis II readies for lunar flyby
stories.description: Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.

stories.title: Iran pushes back against Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Iran's top officials pushed back against a ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

stories.title: US airman rescued after his fighter jet was shot down in Iran
stories.description: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.

stories.title: Trump unleashes curse-filled social media rant at Iran after U.S. rescues colonel
stories.description: In a profanity-laden post on Truth Social, President Trump lashed out at Iran and injected new volatility into the conflict, hours after U.S. forces carried out a high-risk rescue mission.

stories.title: 'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
stories.description: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
stories.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
stories.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

stories.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
stories.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
stories.description: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

stories.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.description: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.title: Israel's perpetual war with Iran may be hard to win with military might alone
stories.description: The war with Iran has not yet resolved any of Israel's regional conflicts in the way its prime minister suggested it would.

stories.title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families
stories.description: People in Iran describe angry scenes and tense relationships as rifts open up over the war.

stories.title: Trump says Iran could be 'taken out' on Tuesday
stories.description: U.S. President ​Donald Trump on Monday told reporters that Iran could be taken out in one night, "and that night might be tomorrow night," warning Tehran it had to make a deal by Tuesday night or face wider bombing raids. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.

stories.title: Trump ramps up threats to devastate Iran as he touts rescue mission
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said Monday he would lay waste to every bridge and power plant in Iran if it fails to bend to his demands, as he touted the high-risk operation that rescued two downed airmen.

stories.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes hit south Beirut, country's south
stories.description: An Israeli strike hit Beirut's southern suburbs on Monday as Israel's army said it was targeting Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, while Lebanon reported broad strikes in the country's south.

stories.title: Donald Trump issues expletive threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: Kethevane Gorjestani, France 24 In ternational Affairs Editor, has more.

stories.title: Donald Trump threatens "hell" unless Tehran reopens Hormuz Strait
stories.description: Resa Sayah, our correspondent in Tehran, has more.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill Iran Guards intel chief as Trump deadline looms
stories.description: Israeli strikes killed the intelligence chief of Iran's Revolutionary Guards, as the Islamic republic on Monday defied threats from US President Donald Trump to devastate civilian infrastructure if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: War in the Middle East: At least four killed in strike on Haifa
stories.description: Iranian missiles hit the northern Israeli city of Haifa, where four people were found dead in the rubble of a residential building.

stories.title: Iran wartime killings increase as another protester hanged
stories.description: Iran on Monday executed a 23-year-old man, Ali Fahim, who was convicted in connection with January's nationwide protests, marking the latest in a wave of politically charged hangings. Rights groups say Fahim and others faced torture, unfair trials and lack of access to legal coun...

stories.title: US president Donald Trump warns Tehran to reopen Hormuz or face "hell"
stories.description: Iran and the United States received a draft proposal late Sunday calling for a 45-day ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
stories.description: Iranian Red Crescent footage shows damage in Iran, as rescuers search for survivors after US-Israeli strikes hit homes.

stories.title: North Korea keeping Iran at arm’s length, reports Seoul
stories.description: South Korea says Pyongyang is wary as it seeks to renew diplomatic dialogue with the US.

stories.title: Hegseth says strikes on Iran increasing as Hormuz deadline looms
stories.description: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth said Monday will be the “largest volume of strikes” on Iran since the war began.

stories.title: Video: Iran’s top university hit in US-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Footage shows damaged research buildings at Tehran’s Sharif University following a US-Israeli strike.

stories.title: Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive
stories.description: Several members of US Congress question president's fitness for office after his expletive-laden Easter Sunday message.

stories.title: How Asia became ground zero for the oil crisis
stories.description: As the Iran war disrupts oil flows, fuel shortages hit Asia first. Is this the start of a wider global energy crisis?

stories.title: Trump warns deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war
stories.description: US president says proposal not enough to avert threat to infrastructure should Iran fail to open Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump praised airman’s rescue in Iran during White House Easter event
stories.description: Donald Trump praised the rescue of a downed American airman in Iran during remarks at the White House Easter Egg roll.

stories.title: Libya’s oil disputes mirror Hormuz crisis, fuel European energy fears
stories.description: Libyan oil sector proxy wars occur during Hormuz tensions, creating dangerous supply vulnerabilities for global markets.

stories.title: Where are Iran’s power plants that Trump has threatened to destroy?
stories.description: US President Trump threatens to destroy power plants, bridges by 8pm Tuesday if Iran doesn't open the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: In maps and photos: Five Iranian civilian bridges at risk of US strikes
stories.description: A look at the multimillion-dollar mega structures that could be threatened by Trump's 'Bridge Day' ultimatum.

stories.title: The US could still try to play the ethnic card in Iran
stories.description: But past experience and current geopolitical realities show such a strategy is doomed to failure.

stories.title: How US operation to rescue air officer from Iran unfolded
stories.description: President Trump says US service member whose fighter jet was shot down over Iran is rescued during two raids.

stories.title: Has Trump confirmed Iran’s claim that protesters were US-armed?
stories.description: US president says Washington armed Kurdish Iranian opposition groups during the January demonstrations.

stories.title: Iran threatens Bab al-Mandeb closure: How would that affect world trade?
stories.description: If the chokepoint were shut, along with the Strait of Hormuz, a quarter of the world's energy supply would be blocked.

stories.title: Trump says Vance, Witkoff, Kushner talking with intermediaries in Pakistan; could ‘take down Iran tomorrow’
stories.description: US President Donald Trump asserted on Monday that Iran could be taken out in one night, “and that night might be tomorrow night”, even as he said shortly afterwards that US officials, including Vice President JD Vance, was talking to intermediaries in Pakistan. His statements fro...

stories.title: Iran conveys to Pakistan its rejection of US ceasefire proposal: report
stories.description: Iran has conveyed its ​response to the ‌US proposal for ending the war ​to intermediary Pakistan, rejecting ​a ceasefire and emphasising ⁠the necessity ​of a permanent end ​to the war, the official IRNA news ​agency said on ​Monday. The Iranian response consisted of 10 clauses, i...

stories.title: Trump calls opening of Hormuz Strait 'very big priority'
stories.description: The US president also suggested that if Washington takes control of the Strait of Hormuz, it may well charge a toll for passage through it

stories.title: Trump hopes he doesn't have to destroy more of Iran's infrastructure
stories.description: The US president that he believed Iran was negotiating in good faith

stories.title: Trump says he would prefer to seize Iran’s oil, but Americans want US forces home
stories.description: The US president added that the American people would like to see America win

stories.title: Trump threatens to destroy all Iran’s bridges, power plants
stories.description: The Islamic Republic will have nothing, the US leader noted

stories.title: US leader says he does not consider destruction of civilian facilities in Iran criminal
stories.description: Allowing a sick country with demented leadership to have a nuclear weapon is a war crime, Donald Trump said

stories.title: US could leave Iran 'right now,' there are many options — US leader
stories.description: Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon, Donald Trump emphasized

stories.title: US, Israel lost 11 aircraft while searching for F-15 pilots in Iran — MWM
stories.description: As MWM notes, this scale of losses demonstrates the significant risks associated with operating aircraft over Iranian territory

stories.title: Israel attacks several airports in Tehran — IDF
stories.description: "Dozens of Israeli Air Force fighter jets struck numerous aerial assets, including aircraft and helicopters, as well as additional military infrastructure at three airports across Tehran: Bahram Airport, Mehrabad Airport, and Azmayesh Airport," the Israel Defense Forces said

stories.title: Forty-five day truce just one of many ideas US, Iran discussing — White House
stories.description: According to the official, the truce has not been approved by US President Donald Trump

stories.title: Central Israel comes under missile attack from Iran, explosions heard in Tel Aviv
stories.description: hortly before that, the Israel Defense Forces said that it had identified missile launches from Iran

stories.title: Iranian army ready to fight as long as leaders deem necessary — agency
stories.description: The way of conducting military operations is structured in such a way that it is possible to wage war in the long term, army representative Mohammad Akraminia said, as quoted by Fars

stories.title: Zaporozhye plant staff ready to share best practices with Iran's Bushehr plant
stories.description: Communications Director Yevgenia Yashina said it is good to know how to ensure reactor safety in a situation where there's no power, how to manage the plant in the face of external threats, protecting cooling systems

stories.title: All countries to pay toll for passing through Strait of Hormuz — agency
stories.description: According to the report, the new rules would strictly apply to all non-hostile countries, including friendly ones, as part of Iran’s strategy to partially offset damage from enemy aggression by collecting a security toll in the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
